[[Mechanics of Soft Tissue#viscoelasticity|viscoelastic]] materials characterised by behaviours aka Time/Rate Dependent factors:

1. Creep:  constant loading for gradual lengthening

e.g. tendons ligaments, slowly stretch under constant load, slowly return to original length after load removed

![](data:image/png;base64,)

2. Stress relaxation: Decrease force required to keep tissue at constant length

e.g. when tissue stretched and HELD, less force required to keep at that length as time passes

![](data:image/png;base64,)

3. Strain-rate sensitivity: Tissues respond differently to loads applied at different speeds

e.g.(rapid loading) High peak force can be applied, creep longer

Vs  (Slow loading) results in lower force but quicker creep

High intensity  vs Low intensity  stretch

![](data:image/png;base64,)

4. Hysteresis: When force applied then removed, path of loading & unloading is not same due to energy loss

e.g. not all energy used in lengthening is recovered during shortening

![](data:image/png;base64,)
